data.title: Zumiez outlines Q2 sales guidance of $207M–$214M amid sourcing diversification and private label gains
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Zumiez Inc. (ZUMZ) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Richard M. Brooks expressed confidence in Zumiez's continued momentum, noting, "Comparable sales for the company grew 5.5%, marking our fourth consecutive quarter of positive comparable sales growth." He emphasized that results "exceed the high end of our guidance ranges for both sales and profitability, excluding a onetime legal sentiment."
  • Brooks highlighted the company's focus on diversifying its sourcing base, stating efforts to "meaningfully reduce our exposure to China by the end of 2025," and underscored continued investments in private label expansion, which reached 30% of total sales in Q1 2025, up from 23% in 2023.
  • Strategic priorities for 2025 include accelerating top line expansion through new product launches, maintaining profitability with cost controls and store closures, and leveraging a strong balance sheet to support growth and shareholder returns. Brooks detailed, "The first quarter this year, we bought back 1.8 million shares or 9.4% of the company...and announced a new buyback plan authorized by our Board for an additional $15 million."
  • CFO Christopher Codington Work reported, "First quarter net sales are $184.3 million, up 3.9% from $177.4 million in the first quarter of 2024. Comparable sales were up 5.5% for the quarter."

Outlook

  • For Q2 2025, Work stated, "We are anticipating total sales to be between $207 million and $214 million for the 13 weeks ending August 3, 2025, representing a negative 2% to positive 2% sales change from the prior year. Comparable sales growth...is expected to be between negative 1% and positive 3%."
  • Product margin is expected to increase from Q2 last year, while consolidated operating loss is projected between $0.7 million and $4 million, compared to a loss of $0.4 million in the prior year. Work added, "We anticipate loss per share will be between $0.09 and $0.24 compared to a loss of $0.04 in the prior year."
  • For the full year 2025, management reiterated an expectation of year-over-year sales growth despite store closures negatively impacting sales by $14.7 million, along with a modest increase in product margin and holding SG&A costs flat as a percentage of sales, excluding one-time legal charges.

Financial Results

  • Net sales reached $184.3 million in Q1 2025, with North America contributing $149.7 million and other international markets $34.6 million. North America net sales increased 4.9% year-over-year, while international declined 0.2%.
  • Gross profit was $55.3 million (30% of sales), up from $51.9 million (29.3% of sales) in Q1 2024. SG&A expense was $75.2 million, or 40.8% of net sales, including a $2.9 million one-time legal settlement.
  • Operating loss was $19.9 million, compared to $20.2 million last year. Net loss was $14.3 million, or $0.79 per share, including the legal settlement. Cash and current marketable securities were $101 million as of May 3, 2025, with no debt.
  • Inventory stood at $149.9 million, up 2.1% from last year. The company repurchased 1.8 million shares in Q1 at a total cost of $25.2 million.

Q&A

  • Mitchel John Kummetz, Seaport Research Partners, asked for detail on China exposure, tariffs, and mitigation strategies. Work responded, "When we ended 2024...roughly 50% of our product was coming out of China. That percentage has been pretty consistent in the first quarter. That said, we expect to see a meaningful decrease as we move through the year...as we get towards the end of the year, we think we'll probably 30% or even potentially lower in product coming out of China."
  • Kummetz followed up on the sustainability of product margin growth despite tariff impacts. Work explained it would be achieved through a combination of sourcing, bundling, and price adjustments, stating, "We think we'll have the opportunity to still grow product margin is our strategy at this point...we continue to think we can still grow product margin."
  • On the international business, Kummetz referenced a "14.8%" comp decline in May. Work confirmed and detailed ongoing focus on product newness, inventory management, and cost controls, while acknowledging, "May was below our plan...we've started a little bit of a hole here for 2025."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was probing and slightly negative, focusing on exposure to tariffs, international performance, and margin sustainability. Questions pressed for clarity on the impact of macroeconomic headwinds and execution of cost management.
  • Management maintained a confident but cautious tone in prepared remarks, using phrases like "I'm confident in our ability to continue delivering value" and "our path forward is clear." During Q&A, responses were detailed but acknowledged challenges, with phrases such as "we'll just keep monitoring where it goes from here" and "May was below our plan."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's sentiment remains optimistic but with increased caution due to heightened macroeconomic and regulatory uncertainty, particularly regarding tariffs and international performance. Analyst tone remains consistently skeptical, especially toward non-North American operations.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Sales and comparable sales growth continued in Q1 2025, but the rate of growth moderated compared to the strong Q4 2024 performance, and gross margin improvement was less pronounced.
  • Management's focus shifted more heavily to sourcing diversification and tariff mitigation, which was less prominent in the previous quarter's discussion.
  • Private label expansion accelerated, now comprising 30% of sales versus 28% at year-end.
  • Guidance language for Q2 and full year 2025 is more cautious, reflecting additional uncertainty from tariff developments and international performance, particularly in Europe.
  • Store closure and cost control strategies continue, but with heightened vigilance on expense management and cash preservation.
  • Analyst concerns remained focused on tariffs and international profitability, consistent with the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Macroeconomic uncertainty and evolving global trade policy remain key risks, with management highlighting the potential impact of tariffs on supply chains and consumer confidence.
  • International performance, particularly in Europe, is cited as a challenge, with May comps down significantly and ongoing efforts to drive profitability.
  • A one-time legal settlement increased SG&A expense in the quarter.
  • Management is actively working to reduce China sourcing exposure and is monitoring further tariff developments closely.
  • Store closures and inventory management are ongoing priorities to mitigate risks to profitability and cash flow.

Final Takeaway

Zumiez management highlights resilience in North America, continued private label expansion, and proactive sourcing diversification as key drivers supporting performance amid a volatile macro and trade environment. The company expects Q2 sales between $207 million and $214 million and aims for modest margin gains, while cautioning that tariffs and international headwinds create ongoing uncertainty. Leadership remains focused on disciplined expense management, cash preservation, and maintaining operational flexibility in response to external challenges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: IDT targets full-year $126M adjusted EBITDA with NRS, BOSS Money, and AI-powered net2phone growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: IDT Corporation (IDT) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Samuel Jonas described the third quarter as "solid with strong year-over-year gains while slightly softer than our second quarter, in part because of expected seasonal factors." He highlighted a 133% year-over-year increase in consolidated income from operations, a 57% increase in consolidated adjusted EBITDA, and a 290% increase in EPS. Jonas pointed to NRS's 23% recurring revenue growth, driven by a 37% jump in merchant services and a 33% rise in SaaS fees, which offset a 12% drop in advertising and data revenue. He stated, "We continue to focus on developing new offerings that leverage the NRS platform to enable retailers to compete more effectively with large retail chains." Jonas also emphasized BOSS Money's 27% increase in transactions and 25% revenue growth, noting the shift toward higher gross profit per transaction and larger send amounts. At net2phone, Jonas detailed the launch of AI agents and the upcoming "Coach" service, saying, "We think will be very successful." For Traditional Communications, Jonas said, "income from operations and adjusted EBITDA both jumped over 30% year-over-year to $17.3 million and $19.3 million, respectively, underscoring that this segment continues to be a long-term cash generator." Jonas concluded, "we continue to scale our 3 primary growth businesses and their operating leverage in combination with the resilient contributions of our Traditional Communications businesses are driving expansion in IDT's cash generation and earnings."
  • CFO Marcelo Fischer stated, "Gross profit increased 15% year-over-year, reflecting increased contributions from each of our 4 reporting segments and was just under last quarter's record level. Our gross profit margin reached another record high of 37.1%." Fischer also noted, "Traditional Communications adjusted EBITDA margin for Q3 grew to 9.2% from 6.7% 1 year ago." He discussed a $1.4 million bad debt expense provision relating to a programmatic platform client and explained a $0.5 million compensation cost from the purchase of deferred stock units from NRS employees. Fischer confirmed, "Given our results to date, we remain fully on track to meeting that goal" of doubling first half adjusted EBITDA to $126 million for the full fiscal year.

Outlook

  • IDT's leadership confirmed guidance for full year fiscal 2025, reiterating, "we expected to double our first half $63 million adjusted EBITDA total for the full year to $126 million." Fischer said, "Given our results to date, we remain fully on track to meeting that goal." Jonas indicated ongoing investment in new offerings for NRS, BOSS Money, and net2phone, with specific focus on AI-powered services and delivery integrations. No new long-term targets or numerical projections beyond the adjusted EBITDA goal were disclosed. Fischer added, "we are now in the midst of our fiscal 2026 budgeting process, and I look forward to providing guidance for our next fiscal year during our fourth quarter earnings call in late September."

Financial Results

  • Jonas reported, "NRS' largest vertical, merchant services," saw 37% revenue growth, with SaaS fees up 33%. NRS generated $32 million in adjusted EBITDA over the past 12 months. BOSS Money transactions increased by 27% and revenue by 25%. The Fintech segment posted over $5 million in adjusted EBITDA, up from $244,000 a year ago. net2phone's adjusted EBITDA margins reached 15%, and subscription revenue increased 7% to $21.5 million. In Traditional Communications, income from operations reached $17.3 million and adjusted EBITDA $19.3 million. Fischer said, "Gross profit increased 15% year-over-year... just under last quarter's record level." IDT ended the quarter with $224 million in cash, cash equivalents, and current investments, up from $171 million, largely due to BOSS Money's weekly working capital cycle. Fischer disclosed the repurchase of 6 million employee-owned shares to satisfy tax obligations.

Q&A

  • Unidentified Analyst asked about NRS terminal revenue and sales strategy. Jonas responded, "more effort was put on bringing both new as well as existing retailers that don't yet have our merchant processing to take it this quarter, and that was definitely helped by many of the new salespeople that we brought on."
  • On net2phone's AI Agent customer profile, Jonas explained, "Most of the customers right now are existing net2phone customers... we're making a bigger effort in the future to sort of verticalize it into some of those areas."
  • Regarding the BOSS Money earnings program, Jonas said, "I'm not honestly sure about the results of the program so far. So I don't feel comfortable commenting."
  • In response to M&A strategy, Jonas stated, "we are definitely looking at acquisitions. We believe that you have to pay the right price to really extract the most value out of an acquisition."
  • On capital allocation, Fischer said, "we believe that repurchasing our stock is a good way of allocating our capital... At the same time, okay, we protect our cash to be able to deploy it towards growing the business towards acquisitions."
  • William Vaughan, Corient, asked about the pace of NRS additions. Jonas replied, "I don't know if I would agree that it's a slowing pace... the numbers are still very strong."
  • Vaughan inquired about food delivery integration; Jonas provided technical details and said, "there's a lot of features and functionalities that it has that help get more orders."
  • Vaughan asked about BOSS Money growth and profit maximization. Jonas replied, "It's on both. I mean what I would say is this, there is a dynamic happening... year-over-year, like our send volume is up 40%."
  • Alexander Rohr, Emmett, inquired about NRS ads business after reducing exposure to a major client. Jonas said, "we are definitely seeing better numbers so far this quarter than we did last quarter... our other ad partners are making up quite a fair share of it."
  • Rohr asked about net2phone EBITDA margin potential. Jonas explained, "it's not a simple question to answer... my own personal opinion is that they should be accretive by a substantial amount, but it might take a year or 2."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a positive tone, with congratulatory remarks and probing on growth pacing, new products, and capital allocation. Questions were constructive, seeking clarity on growth drivers and margin potential.
  • Management was confident but occasionally cautious, especially around new initiatives' results and M&A. Jonas frequently emphasized ongoing investments, future plans, and the strength of current results, with statements such as "we expect it to get a little bit more verticalized over the next year or so."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment remained optimistic, while management continued to express confidence but highlighted operational caution regarding acquisitions and capital allocation.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for adjusted EBITDA remains unchanged, with management reiterating the full-year $126 million target. The focus on operational leverage and high-growth business scaling is consistent with previous remarks.
  • The introduction of AI-powered services at net2phone and delivery integrations at NRS were highlighted as new strategic initiatives this quarter.
  • Analysts sustained their focus on growth drivers, margin expansion, and capital allocation. Management's tone was similar to last quarter, with continued emphasis on discipline in investment and capital returns.
  • Key metric changes include further growth in NRS recurring revenue, BOSS Money digital transaction volume, and continued margin improvements across segments.

Risks and Concerns

  • Fischer disclosed a $1.4 million bad debt expense provision related to a programmatic ad platform client, along with a deliberate reduction in sales to that client to manage receivables risk.
  • Management addressed the need for caution in capital allocation, balancing share repurchases with acquisition opportunities.
  • Jonas noted ongoing assessment of pricing optimization for BOSS Money, acknowledging that customers are sending fewer, larger transactions, which may impact growth and profitability dynamics.
  • Both Jonas and Fischer highlighted the impact of seasonality and working capital cycles, particularly in BOSS Money.

Final Takeaway

IDT reported strong year-over-year growth in income from operations, adjusted EBITDA, and EPS, with leadership reiterating confidence in reaching the full-year $126 million adjusted EBITDA target. The company advanced new initiatives in AI-powered services, online ordering, and delivery integrations, while maintaining discipline in capital allocation and M&A strategy. Management addressed operational risks, including receivables exposure and seasonality, and underscored the continued expansion and profitability of its core business segments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: US Steel-Nippon merger seen closing by deal deadline later this month - Bloomberg
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) and Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NISTF) (OTCPK:NPSCY) are on pace to finalize their $14B merger, as talks on the deal between the companies and the Trump administration are ongoing and expected to reach a conclusion before a June 18 deal deadline, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

The companies, their investors and advisors to the deal are still awaiting final terms on Nippon Steel's (OTCPK:NISTF) (OTCPK:NPSCY) takeover of US Steel (NYSE:X), but talks with the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States are progressing and poised to be finalized before the deal deadline, according to the report.

While final details have not been fully ironed out and talks are ongoing, the agreement likely will include a requirement that US Steel (X), as a subsidiary, retain its headquarters in Pittsburgh, provisions for a U.S. management team, a majority of U.S. nationals on the board, and U.S. government approval of "key" board positions, and possibly an American CEO.

Separately, Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NISTF) (OTCPK:NPSCY) and the Trump administration on Thursday asked a U.S. appeals court to extend a pause in their litigation for eight days, to give the two sides more time to reach a deal.

US Steel's (X) recent stock performance indicates investor optimism that the deal will succeed, as shares have mostly traded above $53 since May 27 - close to Nippon Steel's (OTCPK:NISTF) (OTCPK:NPSCY) $55/share all-cash offer first made in mid-December 2023.

data.title: Banks cut oil price forecast to below $60 but market fundamentals seen rebounding
data.description:
More than two dozen banks have cut their 2025 forecasts for oil prices in the wake of OPEC's move to accelerate production hikes later this summer, according to a new survey by corporate law firm Haynes Boone.

The survey of 28 banks sees oil averaging $58.30/bbl this year, after the same banks projected 2025 oil prices averaging $61.89/bbl last fall.

The price drop is largely attributed to the increased production from OPEC+ and fairly stable demand levels, as well as the dramatic decline in prices seen in April following the Trump administration's tariff announcement.

While oil prices have shifted in the short term, the survey's long-term forecasts remain close to past projections in the $56-$57/bbl range in the early 2030s, suggesting the banks view recent economic changes as temporary.

Crude oil futures rose Thursday following news that the U.S. and China agreed to more trade talks following a phone call between President Trump and China's President Xi, sparking hopes for eventual increased oil demand expectations in both countries.

Escalating U.S.-China trade tensions have prompted fears of a recession and concerns over a softening of oil demand expectations, added by reports that Saudi Arabia is seeking another significant production hike.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for July delivery ended +0.8% to $63.37/bbl, and front-month August Brent crude (CO1:COM) finished +0.7% to $65.34/bbl, the third gain in the past four sessions for both benchmarks, but front-month Nymex July natural gas (NG1:COM) closed -1% to $3.677/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Greif's cost-cutting plan paying off, on track for $100M in savings by 2027, CEO tells WSJ
data.description:
Greif's (NYSE:GEF) strategy to streamline operations is paying off for now, positioning the company for significant growth when economic conditions improve, CEO Ole Rosgaard said Thursday, after reporting FQ2 adjusted earnings that topped estimates.

Q2 net income jumped 43% Y/Y to $68.7M, or $1.19/share, $0.06 better than the $1.13 analyst consensus estimate, and revenues edged 1.1% higher Y/Y to $1.39B, but missed the $1.42B analyst consensus.

Greif (NYSE:GEF) is making steady progress on its cost optimization program, Rosgaard told The Wall Street Journal, as the company expects to achieve up to $25M in savings by the end of the current fiscal year and remains on track to reach $100M in total savings by the end of FY 2027.

The company is pursuing deals in growing sectors such as pharmaceuticals, food and beverage, flavor and fragrance, and agricultural chemicals, the CEO said, noting that these are areas where improved performance is helping it gain market share and pass higher costs along to customers.

Greif (GEF) operates ~250 plants in more than 40 countries and sells most products locally, cushioning it from the effects of tariffs, but the company remains exposed to higher input costs for products such as steel, Rosgaard said.

data.title: Sunnova Energy slashes workforce by 55% as subsidiary files for bankruptcy
data.description:
Sunnova Energy (NYSE:NOVA) disclosed Thursday it plans to lay off 718 employees, or ~55% of its workforce, as it tries to cut costs while grappling with a massive debt pile and weak demand.

The disclosure comes days after subsidiary Sunnova TEP Developer filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in Texas.

Previous reports have said Sunnova Energy (NYSE:NOVA) has been laying the groundwork for a potential bankruptcy filing that could occur in the coming weeks, and the Trump administration last week canceled a $2.9B partial loan guarantee that had been awarded to the company by the U.S. Department of Defense during the Biden administration.

A last-minute change to the budget bill passed by Congress last month would immediately end a 30% tax credit for solar leasing companies that help make rooftop systems affordable to homeowners, which the industry says would lead to a massive drop in the pace of installations.

ETF: (TAN)

data.title: Competing AI hyperscalers, Nebius and CoreWeave, make divergent moves
data.description:
Nebius Group (NASDAQ:NBIS) and CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV), competing artificial intelligence hyperscalers, saw their respective stocks make divergent moves today.

Shares of Nebius surged 17% by the closing bell, while CoreWeave sank 17%.

Shares of both companies have risen rapidly over the past 30 days. Nebius has soared 90% over the past month, while CoreWeave has rocketed 165%. 

Arete Securities initiated coverage of Nebius today with a Buy rating and an $84 price target. The financial services firm pointed to the company's partnership with Nvidia (NVDA), $1.4B cash reserve and 400% year-over-year revenue growth.

Seeking Alpha analyst, The Asian Investor, also gave Nebius a Buy rating and named it a "top AI investment pick."

"The AI start-up has laid out an ambitious plan to grow its revenues aggressively through the scaling of its Data Center operations," The Asian Investor noted today. "In April, the company achieved approximately $310M in annual recurring revenue, showing 244% growth compared to the December 2024 ARR level. The AI start-up expects to grow its ARR business by at least 142% to a range of $750M to $1B by the end of the year."  

Nebius announced earlier this week that it is raising $1B in convertible notes to support its growth strategy in the AI market.

data.title: Dentsply Sirona prices offering of $550M of junior subordinated notes
data.description:
• Dentsply Sirona (NASDAQ:XRAY) on Thursday announced it has priced an offering of $550 million aggregate principal amount of its 8.375% Junior Subordinated Notes due 2055.
  • The offering is expected to close on June 12, 2025.
  • The notes will bear interest at a fixed rate of 8.375% per year until September 12, 2030.
  • Interest on the notes will be payable semi-annually in arrears on March 12 and September 12 of each year, commencing on September 12, 2025.
  • The net proceeds from the offering will be approximately $544.5 million.
  • The company intends to use the proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, including to repay indebtedness outstanding under the company’s existing 364-day $435 million term loan and other short-term indebtedness.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gold forecast to hit new all-time high later this year, report says
data.description:
Gold likely will reach a new all-time high later this year on economic uncertainty stemming from U.S. policy, geopolitical tensions and strong central bank demand, precious metals consultancy Metals Focus said Wednesday in a new report.

Gold's average 2025 price is forecast to rise 35% to $3,210/oz, but a new high "will not be a smooth ride," Metals Focus said, as "sharp corrections and volatility are inevitable given speculator participation... We expect dips to be bought, reinforcing the uptrend."

Even if the worst of a potential global trade war is avoided, tariffs are still expected to be higher than in the past and so uncertainty is likely to persist, the report said.

Central banks worldwide are set to buy 1,000 metric tons of gold in 2025 for a fourth straight year of massive purchases, Metals Focus said, as they continue to shift reserves away from dollar assets.

"Diversification away from the U.S. dollar, whether from a portfolio or reserve management perspective, or for geopolitical reasons, remained the key driver" behind strong central bank demand, according to the report.

Gold futures turned lower in volatile trading Thursday after President Trump said he discussed trade in a call with China's President Xi, deflating some of gold's safe-haven appeal amid indications that the U.S. and China would enter deeper negotiations to settle their trade dispute.

Trump said on social media that the talks focused mostly on trade led and to "a very positive conclusion," while suggesting the export of rare earth minerals also had been discussed.

Gold had posted strong gains last week after Trump accused China of violating a temporary agreement with the U.S., threatening to reignite a trade war between the world's two largest economies.

Meanwhile, silver surged to its highest in 13 years, as investors broaden demand for the safety of precious metals beyond gold.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for June delivery closed -0.7% to $3,350.70/oz, its second loss in three sessions, but front-month June silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +3.4% at $35.689/oz, its highest settlement value since February 28, 2012.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)
